title: The Belmont Report in Practice: Applying Ethical Principles to Modern Human Subjects Research
description: A comprehensive analysis of the Belmont Report's three core ethical principles—Respect for Persons, Beneficence, and Justice—and their critical application in contemporary biomedical and behavioral research. It explores the historical foundation of these principles, offers methodological guidance for their implementation in study design and IRB protocols, and validates their enduring relevance in modern research contexts.
audience: researchers, scientists, and drug development professionals
